title: doctor.py hard-codes .claude-plugin/plugin.json, so every healthy ca-codex install is reported UNHEALTHY (FAIL + exit 1)
evidence: doctor.py check_payload(): `manifest = os.path.join(root, ".claude-plugin", "plugin.json")` then `fail(f"plugin.json unreadable at {manifest}: {e}")` on any open error. The ca-codex plugin ships its manifest at `.codex-plugin/plugin.json` only (verified: `plugins/ca-codex/` contains `.codex-plugin/`, `hooks/`, `ORCHESTRATOR.md` — no `.claude-plugin/`). doctor.py is a byte-identical vendored core file, so on a Codex install this check always FAILs, main() exits 1 with 'verdict: UNHEALTHY — at least one gate cannot function as installed'.
impact: The install-health tool permanently cries wolf on the second host: a correct ca-codex install is reported UNHEALTHY, which (a) misleads users into reinstall loops and (b) trains them to ignore doctor output, masking real dormancy failures — the exact silent-dormancy failure shape doctor exists to catch. check_statusline is also not gated on host.has_statusline (cosmetic on Codex).
recommendation: Move manifest-path resolution onto the host seam (e.g. Host.plugin_manifest_path(), '.claude-plugin/plugin.json' default, '.codex-plugin/plugin.json' on CodexHost), and have doctor consume it; gate check_statusline on host.has_statusline.
acceptance_criteria: doctor.py run inside plugins/ca-codex reports the payload version OK and exits 0 on a correct install
doctor.py under the ca plugin is byte-identical in verdict to before
